primary_concern: Sepsis-3 criteria met with elevated lactate, leukocytosis, and AKI
clinical_summary: Patient demonstrates critical laboratory abnormalities including lactate 4.2 mmol/L (high), persistent thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL), elevated WBC 14.2 K/uL, low hemoglobin 9.8 g/dL, and bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL. AKI progression noted with stage 1 (Cr 2.1) advancing to stage 3 (Cr 8.9). Platelet counts remain below 100 K/uL across multiple timepoints.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2)
Creatinine elevation from 2.1 to 8.9 mg/dL meets KDIGO stage 3 criteria
Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coag score threshold with risk of spontaneous bleeding
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but hemodynamic data unavailable to confirm vasopressor dependence

recommended_actions: Initiate sepsis evaluation per Sepsis-3 protocol including infection source control
Order blood cultures and broad-spectrum antimicrobial coverage
Assess fluid resuscitation status and consider vasopressor readiness
Monitor AKI progression with hourly urine output and creatinine trends
Evaluate for transfusion requirements given anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL)
doctor_handoff: Review cultures, imaging for infection source, and renal replacement therapy readiness. Confirm hemodynamic stability and coagulation studies.

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2, Hgb 9.8, Bilirubin 1.4

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
